reasoning: The LHCb B-decay anomalies (which previously hinted at lepton universality violation at ~3.1 sigma) were resolved in late 2022 when an updated analysis with more data and improved systematic error control found results completely consistent with the Standard Model. Other anomalies generally remain well below the strict 5-sigma threshold and progress in collecting and analyzing the vast amount of data required for a 5-sigma discovery is slow. Given that the primary experimental hint has evaporated, the probability of a 5-sigma peer-reviewed falsification of lepton universality before 2026 is extremely low.
